summary: Lint core: Workspace loader, Diagnostic/Severity types, whole-workspace Rule trait, runner + miette rendering, error-severity drives non-zero exit.

new.description: Workspace { skills: Vec<Skill> } + Workspace::load(dir) walking <ws>/skills/* via Skill::from_surface_dir; Severity { Error, Warn }; Diagnostic { severity, rule, artifact, message, span: Option<...> } (miette Diagnostic); trait Rule { fn check(&self, ws: &Workspace) -> Vec<Diagnostic>; } taking the WHOLE workspace; run(ws, rules: &[Box<dyn Rule>]) -> Vec<Diagnostic> rendered with miette; helper reporting whether any Error fired (for exit code).

edit.description: Add `pub mod check;`. (Also touched by IMPORT — the two share this file, so the fanout partition serializes them.)

tests.asserts: Workspace::load reconstructs skills from a written surface; run with an empty rule set yields no diagnostics; a synthetic error-severity Diagnostic makes the any-error helper return true while warn-only does not.

acceptance: Engine loads a workspace and runs an injectable rule set; clippy + fmt clean.
notes: Unblocked: SKILL-IR shipped (Skill::from_surface_dir on disk). Rule trait takes &Workspace (not a single artifact) so slice-2 cross-artifact rules slot in with no signature change (rust.md). Runner takes rules as a slice so rule registration lives in the CLI, keeping engine and rules disjoint modules.

summary: The 10 slice-1 skill rules (mechanical string/structure checks) + one deliberately-broken fixture per rule.

new.description: Impl the table's 10 rules as Rule structs: frontmatter-valid, name-format, name-matches-dir, description-length (errors); description-third-person, description-has-trigger, description-has-anti-trigger, body-length, refs-one-level-deep (warns); companion-refs-resolve (error). Export all_rules() -> Vec<Box<dyn Rule>>.
One minimal broken skill per rule (and a clean control) that trips exactly its target rule.

tests.asserts: each rule fires on its broken fixture and does NOT fire on clean input; severities match the spec table; companion-refs-resolve reports an error for a body referencing a missing file.

acceptance: Each of the 10 rules has a fixture proving it fires and a clean case proving it stays silent.
notes: All checks are std string/char/path ops over the byte-faithful body + frontmatter — no regex/comrak (outside the CLAUDE.md sanctioned set). Reserved words for name-format: anthropic, claude. Deferred heuristic rules (gerund naming, no-op detection, Leitwort, ToC) are out of slice 1 per the spec — do not add them.

summary: Make the import/check subcommand stubs real: clap args, defaults, dispatch into import::run and check::run with all_rules(), error-driven exit code.

edit.description: Import { harness_path: PathBuf, --into <workspace> default ./.author }; Check { workspace: Option<PathBuf> default ./.author }. Dispatch: Import -> import::run; Check -> load Workspace, check::run(ws, &rules::all_rules()), render diagnostics, exit non-zero if any error-severity fired. Keep main a thin clap dispatch (logic stays in lib).

tests.asserts: import then check over a fixture exits zero on a clean skill and non-zero when an error-severity rule fires; --into / workspace defaults resolve to ./.author.

acceptance: `author import <fixture> --into <tmp>` then `author check <tmp>` runs end to end with the documented exit semantics.
notes: Depends on IMPORT, LINT-ENGINE and LINT-RULES all present (blockedBy names LINT-RULES, the last upstream). CLI is the rule-registration site, so engine and rules stay decoupled. No new dep; drive lib functions (assert_cmd optional, would need a Cargo edit).

summary: Slice-1 acceptance: insta snapshots of import + check over a trimmed coordinate fixture, plus the import-idempotence (no-diff) check.

new.description: insta snapshot of the import surface over a trimmed copy of the coordinate skill (stable across re-runs); insta snapshot of check diagnostics over the rules fixtures (the expected diagnostic set); assert re-running import yields no diff.
Trimmed real-shaped coordinate skill (SKILL.md + a companion) used as the import golden fixture.

tests.asserts: import surface snapshot is byte-stable across two runs; check over the fixtures reproduces the expected diagnostic set; import-then-import produces no diff.

acceptance: The slice acceptance from the spec passes: import <fixture> --into <tmp>, check <tmp> reproduces the expected diagnostics, and re-import produces no diff; `cargo test` green.
notes: Drives the lib import/check functions (logic lives in lib per rust.md) so no binary harness/dep is needed. Trimmed fixture only — do not copy the full ~/.claude/skills/coordinate.
